behavior_in_task_description.reason: The instruction names the specific file path (packages/payload/src/collections/operations/update.ts), the condition to change (shouldSaveDraft || isTrashAttempt), the test collection path (test/trash/collections/Posts/index.ts), the config path (test/trash/config.ts), the types file (test/trash/payload-types.ts), field name (localizedField), localization config keys (localization:, locales:), and the exact string 'localizedField?: string | null'. solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: test_fix_applied asserts the exact string 'shouldSaveDraft || isTrashAttempt' in update.ts, and test_update_ts_context checks its position relative to 'versionsWhere'. An alternative correct fix (e.g., using a different variable name, restructuring the conditional, or extracting the logic into a helper function) would fail these tests despite being functionally correct.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: The core fail-to-pass tests (test_fix_applied, test_posts_collection_has_localized_field, test_trash_config_has_localization, test_payload_types_include_localized_field, test_update_ts_context) are ALL text-matching assertions using 'assert X in open(path).read()'. None of them import, call, or execute the code to verify actual behavior. The ESLint and unit test pass-to-pass tests do execute code, but the f2p tests are purely textual.

test_not_tautological.reason: All fail-to-pass tests are string-presence checks in files. An agent can satisfy them by simply inserting the literal strings (e.g., adding 'shouldSaveDraft || isTrashAttempt' as a comment, or appending 'localizedField' text) without actually fixing the bug or understanding the logic.

anti_cheating_measures.reason: The solution file exists at /workspace/payload_repo (accessible via solve.sh path). The agent could read the solution patch or simply grep for the expected assertion strings ('shouldSaveDraft || isTrashAttempt', 'localizedField', etc.) from the test file. Since tests are purely text-matching, the agent can pass by inserting literal strings without understanding the bug.

behavior_in_tests.reason: The instruction describes a behavioral bug (localized field data lost when bulk-trashing drafts), but no test actually executes the bulk trash operation or checks that localized data is preserved. All tests only check for text patterns in source files.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-named (test_fix_applied, test_posts_collection_has_localized_field, etc.) with docstrings indicating fail-to-pass vs pass-to-pass, and grouped logically.

structured_data_schema.reason: The task does not produce structured data output (JSON/CSV/API/DB schema).

no_typos.reason: File paths, variable names, and commands in instruction.md and tests are consistent and correctly spelled.
